You know the ones which suddenly turns the media about gay rights

we need one that speaks truth about gay wrongs

I have deep hatred for localizers who translates characters based on how they think the character is.
Looks at FE translations
tbf theres no winning there
translating is hard english is a hard language
pay enough to get professional translators no?
professional is a.... loose worse
They just gotta release a massive google doc containing the raw TXT of the game and then just let us translate that
it's crazy how Japanese manga scanlators do better than pros because companies are cheep fucks
Localization is a skill intensive field and doesn't pay
So it is what it is
it only doesn't pay becuase companies are cheep fucks
I mean I get this game, it doesn't have a massive budget but any mihoyo game can afford some real translators
There's a difference between translation being hard and outright deleting text or rewriting
I should save my hate. We don't know if we're in the worst timeline yet
deleting and rewriting is necessary to make translations make sense
english doesn't have a lot of verbs that specify how things are done and most verbs only describe the action
yea but you want it done by someone that gets the context, they have to actually know the story/lore characters
how isn't the right word i dont know how to describe it
if you want to preserve the original meaning of a phrase sometimes you need to add a lot of extra text but sometimes being long-winded also changes the intent

it's really troublesome and you have to make the choice of being consistent with meaning but slightly altering the tone or keeping the original tone but changing the meaning a little bit to have it fit
hate because you can, not because its justitfied
translate  破釜沉舟 to fuck it we ball
what does it actually mean?
google says 'burn one's boats'
I'm assuming it means something about leaving no room for retreat
ask one of our resident chinese speakers
That's why translation is a skill intensive field, with too many low quality translators working for shit pay, bringing down the standard
it's ok AGI will save us, just pay CGPT a few billion more
And tbh AI is a better translator than most doing it for cheap now, you only need an editor
this is true!!!
i dont know why but there are a lot of translators who don't know the language they're translating into well
if they're familiar with the language that it's translating into then even if it's not completely accurate it's still grammatically correct and the meaning can still be somewhat inferred
but being inaccurate and having bad grammar makes it really really hard to understand

I feel like we're not on the same page. Slight alterations and keeping the same tone is good
I'm no manga reader who bitch and moans when the anime isn't 1:1.
What I hate is complete rewrite like in fire emblem fate Saizo x Beruka support where the localizer deleted all text and replaces it with "..." because funny Ninjas don't talk 
Meanwhile JP has a normal conversation
that's entirely related to budget
and if it's deep enough no one notices until it's released
lol is that real?
there's an extreme case with anime from like the 80s where they lost the budget half way though so the english VAs just straigt up ad libed the results and it was a huge cult classic
im a fan of meme translations personally
That's just general unprofessional people working with no oversight <:Coffee:1240919421791375380>
I tried learning Chinese a long time ago, but gave up <:flop:1236937860922343466>

So you always want to apply all buffs first

Tulu exalt + red tentacle - > goliath exalt - > tulu 4 cost
Both Goliath exalt and Tulu exalt apply damage buffs so damage cards come after both
And since Goliath is percentage based his goes as the last buff
Tulu exalt and red tentacle order can be interchanged. Gives the same

I just thought of the reason
When something is divided into 3 or 4 parts then it's called thirds and fourths or quarters
That's why people say 1 third or 1 fourth or 1 quarter
When something is divided into 2 parts the parts are called halves and not seconds
So one piece would be one half and not one second

Its the same as most games. Carries, support, defense, enablers.
masking a comp that fits well together
really you just need good synergy
and synergizes. Good comp = Caucus, Naut, Tawil, Hamel for example. They synergize well, offer damage and defense
which is why aequor is so good cuz they more or less can all play off of each other
Bad comp = Miryam, Alva, Panda, Karen
they do nothing for each other in that case
sone units are flex, meaning they help any comp
hAmel, Thais, Tawil, 24, etc can flex to any comp
whereas a unit like say Wanda
nneeds very specific synergies to thrive
despite being very good
well 24 is literally *the* flex unit
thats her entire gimmick

you'll see why

1 core unit (the character who carries the win condition for your team, can be somebody who triggers a lot of poison like gdoll or builds a lot of counter like caecus or wanda or they can simply just do a lot of damage like aurita or tawil) and 3 support characters (not necessarily characters who have the support tag in-game, but characters who assist the core unit in reaching their win condition like tulu increasing tentacle damage for aurita and caecus or additional poison characters to supplement gdoll's triggers. Aside from helping do more damage, supports are also necessary for surviving so usually there will be at least one defense-oriented character like lily or murphy on the team to help buy time to scale power or poison or tentacle damage)
So this is ok then? Buffs tentacle damage which helps caecus and golaith do more damage
sanga wants her own team tbh
she’s a defense unit but plays more like a dps

there are some teams that don't follow the 1 core/3 support format but they're really niche and it's usually stage dependent (or you're just throwing in all of the strongest units and hoping it works)
it's just easier to have 1 character to focus all of your resources (aliemus, arithmetica) on so that's why multi-core isn't as widely used
